content:
Post 1

⛏️ Materials carried the ASX today. Index added 32.60pts (+0.37%) to 8,841.10 — but the real story is gold stocks splitting: KCN surged 15.89% while RRL shed 5.16%. Same sector, opposite outcomes. #ASX #ausbiz

Post 2

🔺 Materials led all sectors at +1.70%. Telcos dragged hardest at -1.20%, Energy -0.57%, Staples -0.56%. Financials and IT barely held green. A narrow, commodity-driven rally with little breadth. #ASX #materials #ausbiz

Post 3

ZIP CO jumped 8.47% to AUD 3.20 today. Buy-now-pay-later catching a bid while Xero fell 3.57% to AUD 68.27. Tech had no single story — it had two contradictory ones running at once. #ASX #fintech #ausbiz

Post 4

🪙 Gold at AUD 5,769.51/oz, Silver AUD 83.67/oz. AUD/USD sits at 0.6977. Precious metals staying elevated in local terms — that FX rate is keeping gold returns padded for ASX-listed producers. #gold #silver #metals #ASX

Post 5

ASX sits 3.93% off its 52-week high. Watch gold in USD terms overnight — AUD 5,769/oz looks strong, but a firmer USD could compress that quickly. Do you buy the gold miners here or wait for the high? #ASX #gold #ausbiz
